WARNING: This data is ONLY for CSE and CS branch.

course_objectives: To introduce key concepts, lifecycle, and tools in data analytics.
To build practical skills in data preprocessing, visualization, and machine learning techniques.
To explore big data technologies such as Hadoop and Spark.
course_outcomes: CO1: Explain key concepts, tools, and applications of data analytics.
CO2: Apply data cleaning and preprocessing using Python libraries.
CO3: Analyze data and visualize insights with BI tools.
CO4: Implement ML algorithms for classification and clustering.
CO5: Explore Big data framework and demonstrate Hadoop processing.

units.title: Introduction to Data Analytics
topics: Characteristics of data, need of data analytics
Key roles for successful analytic projects

units.title: Data Collection, Cleaning & Preprocessing
topics: Data wrangling using Pandas and NumPy

units.title: Exploratory Data Analysis & Visualization
topics: Distributions, mean, standard deviation, median, mode
Confidence intervals, regression analysis, hypothesis testing
Random variables, probability distributions, Bayes Theorem
Power BI & Tableau: dynamic reports and dashboards

units.title: Machine Learning for Data Analytics
topics: Classification: Decision Trees, k-NN, Naive Bayes
Model evaluation: Confusion Matrix, Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-Score

units.title: Big Data and Advanced Analytics
topics: Introduction to Big Data, Characteristics, Challenges
Apache Spark, Pig, Hive, HBase, Sqoop

textbooks: Michael Berthold, David J. Hand - Intelligent Data Analysis, Springer
Anand Rajaraman and Jeffrey David Ullman - Mining of Massive Datasets, Cambridge University Press
John Garrett - Data Analytics for IT Networks, Pearson Education
reference_books: Foster Provost, Tom Fawcett - Data Science for Business, O'Reilly Media
Wes McKinney - Python for Data Analysis, O'Reilly Media
Jiawei Han, Micheline Kamber, Jian Pei - Data Mining: Concepts and Techniques, Morgan Kaufmann
Tom White - Hadoop: The Definitive Guide, O'Reilly Media

course_objectives: To equip students with foundational and advanced ML techniques including data preprocessing, supervised/unsupervised algorithms, and ensemble methods.
To provide hands-on experience in ANN and real-world applications like disease prediction, AutoML, and digit recognition.
course_outcomes: CO1: Preprocess, clean, and transform datasets, implement supervised ML algorithms (KNN, SVM, Regression) using Python.
CO2: Apply unsupervised techniques (clustering, PCA) and feature engineering to real-world datasets.
CO3: Design ensemble models (Random Forest, Stacking) and optimize hyperparameters using AutoML tools.
CO4: Implement ANN models (MLP, RBF) for tasks like digit recognition and music classification.

units.title: Understanding the Basics of ML
topics: Data preprocessing: handling missing values, encoding categorical variables
KNN Classification, Linear Regression, SVM for spam detection
Confusion matrix, performance metrics, data visualization
units.mini_project: Performance Comparison of Classification models on UCI dataset

units.title: Practical Introduction to ML Algorithms
units.mini_project: Feature Selection in Clustering: Comparative Implementation and Performance Analysis

units.title: Ensemble Learning and Advanced Topics
topics: Hyperparameter Tuning: Traditional vs Metaheuristic optimization
Grid search for ML hyperparameter tuning
units.mini_project: Evaluating AutoML and Manual Hyperparameter Tuning for Disease Prediction Systems

units.title: Fundamentals of ANN
topics: Basics of ANN: Single perceptron, perceptron learning rule

textbooks: Tom M. Mitchell - Machine Learning, McGraw-Hill Education, 2013
Ethem Alpaydin - Introduction to Machine Learning, MIT Press 2004
Stephen Marsland - Machine Learning: An Algorithmic Perspective, CRC Press, 2009
Bishop, C. - Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning, Springer-Verlag
M. Gopal - Applied Machine Learning, McGraw Hill Education
reference_books: Python Data Science Handbook - Jake VanderPlas
Ensemble Methods for Machine Learning - Gautam Kunapuli (Manning)
AutoML: Methods, Systems, Challenges - Frank Hutter et al.
Neural Networks and Deep Learning - Charu C. Aggarwal (Springer)

course_objectives: To familiarize students with the modern technologies used in web technologies.
To realize the fundamentals of Flutter & Python web development frameworks.
course_outcomes: CO1: Understand advanced ES6 JavaScript features to develop modular and asynchronous applications.
CO2: Build cross-platform mobile applications by applying Dart and Flutter with OOP and async concepts.
CO3: Design and implement interactive Flutter applications using state management, navigation, and basic animations.
CO4: Build web applications using Flask and Django, utilizing MVC architecture, form handling, and REST API development.

units.title: Advanced ES6 JavaScript
topics: Variable declarations: var vs let vs const
Arrow functions: syntax, single-line and multi-line
Array destructuring and object destructuring, default values
then(), catch(), and basic error handling

units.title: Fundamentals of Flutter and Dart Programming
topics: Introduction to Cross-Platform Development and Flutter framework
Installing Flutter SDK, Android Studio Setup
Dart Basics: Variables, Data Types, Control Flow, Functions, Scope
OOP & Async: Classes, Objects, Inheritance, Mixins, Future, async/await
Stateless vs. Stateful Widgets, Container, Row, ListView, Image

units.title: Input, Navigation UI and Animations
topics: Handling Input Forms, Handling Taps and Events
Android App Deployment and Publishing on Google Play

units.title: Web Development with Python: Django & Flask
topics: Flask: Installation, basic routing, request and response handling
Django: MVC architecture, installation, project setup
Connecting to databases, Creating models, migrations
Django Views & Templates, URL routing, template rendering, form handling

textbooks: JavaScript: The Definitive Guide, David Flanagan
Flutter and Dart: Up and Running, Roopal Khurana and Dr. Deepti Chopra
Flutter for Beginners by Alessandro Biessek
Flask Web Development: Developing Web Applications with Python, Miguel Grinberg
reference_books: Flutter and Dart Cookbook: Developing Full-Stack Applications for the Cloud by Richard Rose
